decks.notes: The main concept of this deck is to establish a strong control over the game by using artifacts to create a prison-like environment for the opponent. Cards like Ugin, Karn, and Trinisphere help to control the board while Planar Nexus and Urza's lands provide mana acceleration. The inclusion of powerful Eldrazi creatures like Emrakul and Ulamog serves as win conditions. With cards like Pithing Needle and The One Ring, the deck aims to disrupt the opponent's strategy while utilizing artifacts like Grim Monolith and Candelabra of Tawnos for additional resources and mana. This deck is designed to outlast and overpower opponents through a combination of control and prison tactics.

The main concept of this deck is to quickly fill up the graveyard with powerful creatures such as Griselbrand and Archon of Cruelty, using cards like Faithless Looting and Unmask, then cheat them into play using Reanimate and Animate Dead. This allows for explosive plays and overwhelming your opponent with powerful creatures early in the game. The deck also includes disruptive cards like Thoughtseize and Cabal Therapy to control the opponent's hand and disrupt their strategy, while cards like Shallow Grave provide additional reanimation options.

The deck focuses on assembling the combo piece Doomsday, which allows the player to stack their deck in a specific order to win the game. It utilizes cards like Dark Ritual and Lion's Eye Diamond to generate mana quickly, while also including disruption like Thoughtseize and Force of Will to protect the combo. Tamiyo, Inquisitive Student and Murktide Regent provide additional card advantage and threats on the board. The deck aims to efficiently set up the Doomsday combo while managing resources and interacting with the opponent's game plan.

The main concept of this Mardu Aggro deck is to overwhelm the opponent with aggressive creatures and disruption spells. The deck focuses on quickly putting pressure on the opponent with efficient creatures like Amped Raptor and Goblin Bombardment, while also disrupting their game plan with cards like Cabal Therapy and Thoughtseize. The addition of powerful planeswalkers like Ajani, Nacatl Pariah and versatile removal spells like Swords to Plowshares helps maintain control of the battlefield. With a mix of creature aggression and disruption, this deck aims to outpace and outplay the opponent in a fast-paced, aggressive manner.

This deck focuses on quickly getting large threats into play from the graveyard using cards like Reanimate, Animate Dead, and Shallow Grave. By discarding a creature like Archon of Cruelty or Griselbrand with cards like Faithless Looting or Unmask, and then reanimating it with spells like Reanimate, the deck can quickly overpower opponents with powerful creatures. The deck also uses disruption spells like Cabal Therapy and Collective Brutality to disrupt opponents' strategies and protect its own game plan. With efficient mana acceleration from Dark Ritual and Lotus Petal, the deck can execute its combo quickly and decisively.

The main goal of this deck is to quickly and efficiently cheat powerful big creatures onto the battlefield using cards like Show and Tell and Sneak Attack. By utilizing these cards, the deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with huge threats such as Griselbrand, Emrakul, the Aeons Torn, and Atraxa, Grand Unifier. The deck also includes cards like Ponder, Brainstorm, and Stock Up to help find key combo pieces and protect the combo with cards like Force of Will and Flusterstorm. Overall, this deck focuses on executing a game-winning combo as quickly as possible.

The deck focuses on quickly and efficiently getting powerful creatures onto the battlefield by using cards such as Show and Tell, Omniscience, and Sneak Attack. The primary goal is to cheat out big threats like Emrakul, the Aeons Torn, and Atraxa, Grand Unifier to overwhelm the opponent and win the game in a single turn. The deck also includes various control and card selection spells like Force of Will, Brainstorm, and Ponder to protect the combo pieces and ensure a smooth execution of the game plan.

The main focus of this UWx Control deck is to disrupt the opponent's game plan while controlling the board using counterspells, removal spells, and powerful planeswalkers like Tamiyo and Teferi. The deck utilizes cards like Force of Will, Stifle, and Wasteland to interact with the opponent's strategies, while also generating card advantage and board presence with cards like Brainstorm, Snapcaster Mage, and Quantum Riddler. The deck aims to consistently answer threats and maintain control of the game until it can establish a dominant position and close out the game efficiently.

This deck focuses on locking down the opponent's options through a combination of artifact-based control elements. By utilizing cards like Karn, the Great Creator and Trinisphere, along with powerful artifacts like Candelabra of Tawnos and Urza's Saga, the deck aims to disrupt the opponent's game plan and establish a prison-like board state. Emrakul, the Aeons Torn provides a powerful win condition, while cards like Grim Monolith and Ugin, the Eye of the Storms help generate additional value. Overall, the deck's strategy revolves around controlling the game and outlasting the opponent.

This Aluren combo deck focuses on quickly getting the Aluren enchantment into play, allowing the player to cast their creatures for free. By using cards like Show and Tell, Acererak the Archlich, and Omniscience, the deck can quickly overwhelm the opponent with powerful creatures like Emrakul, the Aeons Torn and Atraxa, Grand Unifier. The deck also includes control elements like Force of Will and Veil of Summer to protect the combo pieces and disrupt the opponent's game plan. The goal is to assemble the combo and win the game in a single explosive turn.

The key concept of this deck is to maintain tempo and aggression through cheap spells and disruptive cards, while utilizing powerful threats like Dragon's Rage Channeler and Murktide Regent to quickly close out the game. By combining efficient draw spells like Brainstorm and Ponder with interaction like Daze and Force of Will, the deck aims to control the pace of the game and overwhelm the opponent with efficient threats. Wasteland and Mishra's Bauble provide additional value and disruption, making it a versatile and dynamic deck that can adapt to various matchups in Legacy 1 vs 1 format.

This Boros Aggro deck focuses on quick and aggressive creature plays, utilizing cards like Ajani, Nacatl Pariah and Amped Raptor to quickly overwhelm opponents. Swords to Plowshares provides removal, while Goblin Bombardment adds a direct damage element. Thalia, Guardian of Thraben disrupts opponents' strategies, and Flagstones of Trokair helps with land consistency. The deck also includes some utility lands like Karakas and Wasteland. Overall, the deck aims to apply constant pressure and control the board with a mix of strong creatures and disruption spells.

The deck focuses on utilizing resource denial with cards like Wasteland and disrupting the opponent's game plan with counterspells like Force of Will. It also aims to maintain a fast pace through efficient creatures like Dragon's Rage Channeler and disruptive spells like Lightning Bolt. By utilizing tempo plays and aggressive threats, the deck aims to control the pace of the game while gradually chipping away at the opponent's life total. Overall, the deck's strategy revolves around applying pressure and maintaining control of the game to secure a victory.

This deck focuses on quickly deploying threats like Dragon's Rage Channeler and Delver of Secrets while disrupting the opponent's game plan with cards like Daze and Force of Will. It aims to control the game tempo with cheap spells like Brainstorm and Ponder to keep the pressure on the opponent. By utilizing cards like Wasteland and Lightning Bolt to deal with threats, as well as protecting its own creatures, the deck looks to maintain board control and eventually overwhelm the opponent with its aggressive creatures.

The deck focuses on using artifacts and control elements to lock down the opponent's options and resources, while also generating massive mana to cast powerful threats like Emrakul. The key strategy is to establish a proactive prison-like board state by playing disruptive artifacts such as Trinisphere and Karn, the Great Creator, while also utilizing Urza lands and Grim Monolith for ramp. The deck aims to achieve dominance through card advantage and overwhelming the opponent with threats that are difficult to deal with, ultimately securing victory through control and resource denial.

The main focus of this UWx Control deck is to control the board with a mix of countermagic, removal spells, and card advantage. The deck utilizes cards like Force of Will, Back to Basics, and Swords to Plowshares to disrupt the opponent's game plan, while also generating card advantage with cards like Tamiyo, Inquisitive Student and Snapcaster Mage. Murktide Regent provides a powerful threat that can close out the game quickly. The deck aims to outvalue the opponent by efficiently dealing with threats and generating card advantage to come out on top in the late game.
